solution.p: The first step is to subtract terms in order to separate the variable and non-variable terms. \begin{aligned} 6c +5 \amp = c +45 \\ 6c +5\mathbf{{}-c-5} \amp = c +45\mathbf{{}-c-5} \\ 5c \amp = 40 \\ \frac{5c}{5} \amp = \frac{40}{5} \\ c \amp = 8 \end{aligned}

The solution to this equation is 8. To stress that this is a value assigned to c, some report c = 8. We can also say that the solution set is \{8\}, or that c\in\left\{8\right\}. If we substitute 8 in for c in the original equation 6c+5=c+45, the equation will be true. Please check this on your own; it is an important habit.
